purpose: Header-only AudioProcessor that delays its stereo input by N samples using a circular buffer. Auto-inserted by IconMenu::reconnectGraph on shorter lanes to match the longest lane's accumulated latency, providing Plugin Delay Compensation across parallel lanes.
invariants: Fixed stereo I/O — input and output buses are both AudioChannelSet::stereo() and that pairing is required by JUCE's graph for the auto-insertion path.

delayInSamples is clamped to [0, sampleRate*10] in prepareToPlay — guards against misbehaving plugins reporting absurd getLatencySamples() values that would otherwise trigger gigabyte-scale buffer allocations.
buffer size is at least max(maximumExpectedSamplesPerBlock, delayInSamples + 1) — the +1 ensures the read pointer never lands on the just-written sample when delay equals one block size.
processBlock with delayInSamples == 0 is a no-op (early return) — the graph can leave the delay node in place with a zero delay without performance cost.
Channel count in processBlock is min(data.getNumChannels(), buffer.getNumChannels()) so a mono signal flowing through doesn't read past the buffer's end.
gotchas: No editor (hasEditor()==false, createEditor()==nullptr); inserting via PluginWindow::getWindowFor will return nullptr — that's intentional, the delay is invisible to the UI.
No state persistence — getStateInformation/setStateInformation are empty. Delay sample counts are recomputed from scratch on every graph rebuild, never restored from disk.
using juce::AudioProcessor::processBlock; un-hides the double-precision overload so -Woverloaded-virtual stays quiet — we don't implement double precision; JUCE's graph runs in float by default.

delayInSamples is a plain int (not atomic). Audio thread reads it in processBlock but it's only ever written during prepareToPlay (which is called on the message thread before audio starts). Don't write to it from anywhere else.
